data.title: As a DSO plugin developer, reducer schema supports tokens_estimated and tokens_measured fields
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend the reducer compiled-state schema in `ticket_reducer/_state.py` to include two new optional fields: `tokens_estimated` (numeric, default 0) and `tokens_measured` (numeric, default 0). Extend `ticket-edit.sh` allowed fields so agents can write these values via EDIT events.

**Why**: Without a schema foundation, downstream hooks and skills have nowhere to record token counts. Numeric fields also enable Jira sync via Number custom fields (deferred to Epic 83e2-04ae).

**Scope**:
- IN: Add `tokens_estimated` and `tokens_measured` to `ticket_reducer/_state.py` defaults; reducer initializes both to 0 on CREATE, updates on EDIT events.
- IN: Add `tokens_estimated` and `tokens_measured` to `ticket-edit.sh` ALLOWED_FIELDS; numeric validation (integer >= 0).
- IN: `ticket show` default and `--format=llm` outputs surface both fields (shortened keys `te`, `tm` for llm format).
- OUT: Hook and skill integration — separate stories.
- OUT: Jira sync — Epic 83e2-04ae.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket edit <id> --tokens_estimated=12345` writes an EDIT event and the reducer surfaces `tokens_estimated: 12345` in `ticket show <id>`.
  ← Satisfies: "Per-ticket token attribution — reducer schema foundation"
- When this story is complete, `ticket show --format=llm <id>` surfaces `te` and `tm` keys with numeric values.
  ← Satisfies: "Per-ticket token attribution — reducer schema foundation"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Integer-only validation — reject floats, negative numbers, non-numeric strings.
- [Maintainability] Follow the existing EDIT event field pattern; no new event types.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
